title: Washington coast — an honest hard case

detector.model: RUN-30 detector (pre-registered dropPRIOR config), LOEO, preliminary calibration — CAL split only; frozen EVAL-2 untouched

framing: Fireball networks tell us when and where to look; MetDetect answers which radar echoes are the falling debris. Shown: every candidate the detector flags at its 10%-false-alarm operating point, in a window placed on the fireball report — false positives included.
narrative: A fireball off the Washington coast, observed by the KLGX radar through steady stratiform rain. Here the method honestly struggles: nothing clears the detection threshold, and the top-ranked candidate is a rain cluster — with the real, manually verified debris ranked a hair behind it (2nd of ~2,000 candidates in this scene). Weather-embedded falls remain the hard frontier; the hollow rings mark where the debris really is.
